name: Heliod's Sun Censer
description: Bronze censer that burns olive oil in a brilliant white flame. Sheds bright light 15 ft. during a religious ceremony.

name: Thassa's Coral Token
description: A polished sliver of deep-sea coral. Worshippers carry it for safe passage across open water; said to calm rough swells when tossed overboard.

name: Erebos's Obol (coin for the dead)
description: Black bronze coin placed on the tongue of the dead so the Returned can pay their passage. Funerary use only — buying anything else with it is taboo.

name: Nylea's Bowstring (sacred sinew)
description: Bowstring spun from the sinew of a beast slain in fair hunt. Imparts a sense of true aim — once per long rest, reroll a single missed bow attack.

name: Iroas War Laurel
description: Bronze-leafed laurel awarded to victors. Worn into battle, it grants advantage on saves against being frightened for 1 hour.

name: Purphoros Forge Ember (iron vial)
description: Sealed iron vial containing a single never-cooling forge ember. Ignites tinder instantly; shatter to deal 1d4 fire to one creature.

name: Karametra Harvest Wreath
description: Wreath of wheat, olive, and grape. Hung above a doorway, it blesses one meal per day so it counts as a feast for morale.

name: Pharika Antidote Salve
description: Bitter green ointment. Grants advantage on the next saving throw against poison if applied within 1 minute.

name: Mogis Bronze Tag (cursed talisman)
description: Black bronze tag stamped with the bull-mask of Mogis. Carriers feel a slow rage build; useful for swaying minotaur kin, dangerous to bear long.

name: Phenax Silvered Veil-Mask
description: Half-mask of mirrored silver. Wearer gains advantage on Deception checks made while their identity is concealed.

name: Kruphix Astral Lens
description: Smoke-tinted lens framed in starwood. Looking through it, the user can faintly see the constellations even in daylight.

name: Klothys Fate-Threaded Bracelet
description: Bracelet of three colored threads — red, gold, black. The wearer may reroll one d20 once per week, but Klothys takes note.

name: Phial of Nyx Mist
description: Glass phial of swirling violet vapor. Released, it fills a 10-ft. cube with starlit fog (lightly obscured) for 1 minute.

name: Healing Honey of Setessa
description: Thick golden honey from the Setessan hives. Acts as 1 use of a healer's kit and restores 1d4+1 hp when eaten.

name: Cyclops Bile Tonic
description: Foul-smelling brown tonic. Grants advantage on Strength (Athletics) checks for 10 minutes; drinker is poisoned for 1 hour after.

name: Returned-Ward Powder
description: Salt-and-bone powder. A 10-ft. circle drawn with it cannot be crossed by Returned (zombies) for 1 hour.

name: Hydra-blood Elixir
description: Crimson elixir distilled from hydra venom. The drinker regains 1 hp at the start of each of their turns for 1 minute; ends if reduced to 0.

name: Medusa-eye Drops
description: Pale drops squeezed from a slain gorgon's tear duct. Grants the drinker advantage on saves vs. petrification for 24 hours.

name: Olive Oil of Karametra
description: Cold-pressed sacred olive oil. Quality cooking oil; blessed bottles also count as holy oil for funerary rites.

name: Bronze Hoplite Sword
description: Classic xiphos of beaten bronze. 1d6 slashing, light, finesse. Standard polis-issue sidearm.

name: Leonin War-claw Gauntlet
description: Steel gauntlet tipped with leonin-forged claws. Unarmed strikes deal 1d6 slashing.

name: Minotaur Greataxe
description: Massive double-bitted axe sized for a Mogis-touched minotaur. 1d12 slashing, heavy, two-handed. Requires Str 15 to wield without disadvantage.

name: Heliod-blessed Spear
description: Sun-blessed bronze spear with a gilded haft. 1d6 piercing (1d8 two-handed), thrown 20/60. Sheds dim light 5 ft.

name: Aegis Shield of the Polis
description: Round bronze shield embossed with the city's sigil. +2 AC. Polished mirror surface grants advantage on saves vs. a gorgon's gaze (one save only).

name: Bronze Hoplon Shield
description: Heavy round hoplon used by phalanx lines. +2 AC. Locks with adjacent allies' shields — provides half cover to one ally beside you.

name: Akroan Bronze Cuirass
description: Sculpted bronze breastplate of Akros, fitted to the wearer. AC 14 + Dex (max 2). Medium armor, 20 lb.

name: Centaur Longbow
description: Tall recurve longbow shaped for centaur draw. 1d8 piercing, range 150/600, heavy, two-handed.

name: Nylea-string Hunting Bow
description: Shortbow strung with Nylea-blessed sinew. 1d6 piercing, range 80/320. Animal targets have disadvantage on saves vs. the wielder's hunt spells.

name: Skystrike Arrows (12)
description: Dozen arrows fletched with stormcrow feathers. Ignore the disadvantage from long range while it is windy or storming.

name: Quiver of Akros
description: War quiver holding 20 arrows and 4 javelins. Drawing an arrow is part of the attack.

name: Satyr-hide Vest
description: Light leather vest of supple satyr hide. AC 11 + Dex. Grants advantage on Performance checks involving dance.

name: Returned-bone Bracers
description: Bracers carved from bleached Returned bone. +1 to Intimidation checks; unsettling to most folk.

name: Olive-tanned Sandals
description: Soft leather sandals oiled with sacred olive. Quiet on stone — advantage on Stealth on tile or marble.

name: Oracle's Clouded Eye Crystal
description: Milky quartz sphere. Once per day, ask the DM a single yes/no question with 50% truthful answer.

name: Nyx-spun Scroll Case
description: Scroll tube woven of moonlit thread. Holds up to 6 scrolls; contents are immune to fire and water damage.

name: Star-chart of Kruphix
description: Inked star-chart on goat vellum. Advantage on Survival checks to navigate at night.

name: Lampad Starlight Lantern
description: Lantern that burns no oil — sheds dim starlight in a 20-ft. radius when uncovered. Goes dark in sunlight.

name: Phenax Mirror-shard
description: Razor shard of polished obsidian. Catches whispers — advantage on Perception (hearing) for 1 minute when held to the ear.

name: Nyx-touched Spell Focus
description: Crystal pendant veined with starlight. Functions as an arcane focus; grants +1 to spell attack rolls for cantrips only.

name: Triton Deep-Pearl
description: Iridescent pearl from the deep trenches. Held in mouth, lets you breathe water for 10 minutes.

name: Cyclopean Amber Bead
description: Single huge amber bead with an insect from the age of Titans. Worth alone in any jeweler's eye.

name: Heliod Sunburst Ring
description: Gold ring set with citrine sunburst. Wearer adds +1 to saves vs. fear from undead.

name: Returned-coin Necklace
description: Necklace strung with old funerary obols. Cursed reputation — vendors offer 10% less for goods you sell.

name: Meletian Purple Toga
description: Murex-dyed toga of a Meletian senator. +1 to Persuasion in polite Meletian society.

name: Akros War-Cloak
description: Crimson wool cloak of an Akroan soldier. Resists cold weather; counts as cold-weather gear.

name: Setessan Vine Sash
description: Living-vine sash from the temple of Karametra. The vine reties itself if cut.

name: Karametra Olives (sealed jar)
description: One jar of brined Setessan olives. Feeds one person one meal; counts as good rations.

name: Setessan Honey Wine (amphora)
description: Amphora of golden honey wine. 6 servings. Sharing a cup grants advantage on the next Persuasion check with a Setessan.

name: Sacred Fig Loaf
description: Round loaf studded with sacred figs. One meal; counts as a temple offering.

name: Devotion Sigil Tattoo
description: Inked sigil of a chosen god placed on the chest. While devout, the bearer has advantage on saves vs. being charmed by hostile cult magic.

name: Vial of Nightmare Dust
description: Black powder ground from Erebos-touched poppy. Sprinkled on a sleeper, they suffer vivid nightmares (no benefit from the rest).

name: Forged Polis Seal
description: Counterfeit wax seal of a major polis. Illegal — DC 16 Investigation to spot the forgery.

name: Knucklebones of the Returned
description: Set of four bone dice carved from Returned finger-knuckles. Lucky in shady company; cursed in temples.

name: Potion of Supreme Healing
description: Restores 10d4+20 hit points when drunk. (DMG very rare healing potion.)

name: Potion of Speed
description: Drinker gains the effects of the haste spell for 1 minute. No concentration required.

name: Potion of Flying
tags: fly speed equal to walking 1 hr
description: Drinker gains a flying speed equal to their walking speed for 1 hour. Must remain conscious.

name: Oil of Sharpness
description: Coats one slashing or piercing weapon (or 5 pieces of ammunition). For 1 hour the weapon is magical and gains +3 to attack and damage rolls.

name: Potion of Invulnerability
description: Drinker gains resistance to all damage for 1 minute.

name: Potion of Cloud Giant Strength
description: Drinker's Strength score becomes 27 for 1 hour. No effect if Str is already 27 or higher.

name: Potion of Storm Giant Strength
description: Drinker's Strength score becomes 29 for 1 hour. No effect if Str is already 29 or higher.

name: Oil of Etherealness
description: Drinker (or one creature touched) gains the effects of the etherealness spell for 1 hour.

name: Potion of Longevity
description: Drinker's physical age is reduced by 1d6+6 years (minimum 13). Each subsequent use carries a 10% cumulative chance to age 1d6+6 years instead.

name: Potion of Vitality
description: Removes any exhaustion the drinker is suffering and cures any disease or poison affecting them. For 24 hours the drinker regains the maximum number of hit points on any Hit Die spent.

name: Phase Spider Silk-Gland
description: Iridescent gland from a phase spider. Component for potions of ethereal or planar travel.

name: Hydra Tooth (cured)
description: Cured tooth of a Theros hydra. Used in regenerative tonics and Hydra-blood elixirs.

name: Cockatrice Feather
description: Stiff cockatrice feather, packed in oil. Component for petrification-related elixirs.

name: Mandrake Root (whole)
description: Whole screaming mandrake root, sealed in beeswax. Component for healing and sleep potions.

name: Wyvern Antitoxin Vial
description: Distilled wyvern venom, neutralized. Acts as antitoxin: advantage on saves vs. poison for 1 hour.

name: Phoenix Down (one tuft)
description: Single tuft of phoenix down. Component for high-tier healing potions; warm to the touch.

name: Basilisk Eye (preserved)
description: One preserved basilisk eye in saline. Component for Medusa-eye drops and petrify-ward elixirs.

name: Minotaur Horn Shavings
description: Bag of horn shavings from a felled minotaur. Used in stoneskin and stamina draughts.

name: Lampad Tear (1 dram)
description: Single dram of lampad tear, glowing faintly. Component for Nyx-mist and dream potions.

name: Sphinx Lash
description: One eyelash from a Theros sphinx. Used in divination inks and clarity tonics.

name: Akroan Hoplite Xiphos
description: Short straight bronze sword, the standard Akroan infantry sword. 1d6 slashing, light, finesse.

name: Setessan Crescent Sickle
description: Curved harvesting sickle. 1d4 slashing, light. Setessan rangers favor it as an off-hand.

name: Skola Vale Trident
description: Three-pronged trident used by Triton-allied warriors. 1d6 piercing (1d8 two-handed), thrown 20/60.

name: Meletian War-pick
description: Curved war-pick used to punch bronze armor. 1d8 piercing, martial.

name: Phenax Veil-dagger
description: Slim blackened dagger. 1d4 piercing, light, finesse, thrown 20/60. Favored by Phenax cultists.

name: Karametra Throwing Sling
description: Setessan leather sling. 1d4 bludgeoning, range 30/120. Cheap and reliable.

name: Akroan Long Spear (sarissa)
description: Long phalanx spear. 1d6 piercing (1d8 two-handed), reach. Awkward indoors.

name: Heavy Bronze Mace
description: Solid cast-bronze mace. 1d6 bludgeoning, simple. Effective against Returned.

name: Setessan Boar Spear
description: Stout boar spear with a crossbar. 1d6 piercing. Crossbar gives +2 AC against the first charge by a Large+ beast.

name: Centaur Composite Bow
description: Composite longbow sized for centaur draw weight. 1d8 piercing, range 150/600, two-handed.

name: Triton Coral Trident
description: Carved coral trident used in shallows. 1d8 piercing (two-handed). No disadvantage when attacking underwater.

name: Cyclopean Stone Maul
description: Crude two-handed stone maul. 2d6 bludgeoning, heavy. Requires Str 15 or attacks have disadvantage.

name: Linothorax Linen Cuirass
description: Layered linen and resin cuirass. AC 12 + Dex. Light, 8 lb. Common Akroan armor.

name: Bronze Scale Corselet
description: Bronze scales on linen. AC 14 + Dex (max 2). 45 lb. Disadvantage on Stealth.

name: Phalanx Bronze Greaves
description: Pair of bronze shin greaves. +1 AC against opportunity attacks made against your legs (DM call).

name: Setessan Hunter's Leathers
description: Olive-tanned light armor. AC 11 + Dex. Advantage on Stealth in forest terrain.

name: Leonin Mane-cloak
description: Cloak with a leonin's mane sewn to the shoulders. +1 to Intimidation against beasts.

name: Triton Scale Mail
description: Scales of pearl-mussel sewn on leather. AC 14 + Dex (max 2). No swim penalty.

name: Plated Hoplite Panoply
description: Full Akroan hoplite plate. AC 16. Disadvantage on Stealth. Str 13 to avoid speed penalty.

name: Returned-bone Scale
description: Light scales fashioned from Returned bone. AC 13 + Dex (max 2). Wearer has advantage on saves vs. necrotic damage; deeply taboo.

name: Shortsword +1
description: +1 magic shortsword. 1d6+1 piercing, finesse, light. Bonus to attack and damage rolls.

name: Longsword +1
description: +1 magic longsword. 1d8+1 slashing (1d10 two-handed). Bonus to attack and damage rolls.

name: Heavy Crossbow +1
description: +1 magic heavy crossbow. 1d10+1 piercing, range 100/400. Bonus to attack and damage rolls.

name: Light Crossbow +2
description: +2 magic light crossbow. 1d8+2 piercing, range 80/320. Bonus to attack and damage rolls.

name: Akroan Spear +1
description: +1 magic spear forged in the Akroan war-temples. 1d6+1 piercing (1d8 two-handed), thrown 20/60.

name: Trident of the Shoals +2
description: +2 magic trident blessed by Thassa. No disadvantage when attacking underwater. 1d6+2 / 1d8+2 piercing.

name: Greataxe +1
description: +1 magic greataxe. 1d12+1 slashing, heavy, two-handed. Bonus to attack and damage rolls.

name: Warhammer +2
description: +2 magic warhammer. 1d8+2 bludgeoning (1d10 two-handed). Bonus to attack and damage rolls.

name: Quarterstaff +1
description: +1 magic quarterstaff. 1d6+1 bludgeoning (1d8 two-handed). Bonus to attack and damage rolls.

name: Dagger of Phenax +2
description: +2 magic dagger of woven shadow. 1d4+2 piercing, finesse, light, thrown 20/60. Bonus to attack and damage rolls.

name: Longbow +1
description: +1 magic longbow. 1d8+1 piercing, range 150/600, heavy, two-handed.

name: Shortbow +2
description: +2 magic shortbow. 1d6+2 piercing, range 80/320, two-handed.

name: Mace of the Sun +1
description: +1 magic mace consecrated to Heliod. 1d6+1 bludgeoning. Sheds bright light in 10 ft. against undead, who have disadvantage on saves vs. it.

name: Sling of the Hunter +1
description: +1 magic sling of Nylean make. 1d4+1 bludgeoning, range 30/120. Counts as magical against fiends and beasts.

name: Greatsword +2
description: +2 magic greatsword. 2d6+2 slashing, heavy, two-handed. Bonus to attack and damage rolls.

name: Shield +1 (Polis Aegis)
description: +1 magic shield. While held, AC increases by an additional +1 beyond a normal shield's +2.

name: Studded Leather +1
description: +1 magic studded leather. AC 12 + Dex + 1.

name: Chain Shirt +2
description: +2 magic chain shirt. AC 13 + Dex (max 2) + 2.

name: Wand of the Web (3 charges)
description: Wand with 3 charges; expend 1 to cast web (DC 13). Regains 1d3 charges at dawn. Uncommon-tier reskinned for the Skola Vale.

name: Cloak of Protection
description: Rare. While worn, +1 bonus to AC and saving throws. Requires attunement.

name: Crushed Olive Pit
description: Cheap binding agent for healing salves. Almost every Theros apothecary keeps a sack of these.

name: Dried Asphodel Petals
description: Pale grey petals from the fields near the Underworld's edge. Used in sleep draughts and funerary incense.

name: Vial of Honey-Wine
description: Karametra's sweet binding for tonics. Doubles as a pleasant treat.

name: Powdered Cypress Bark
description: Earthy reagent for restorative balms. The cypress grows thickest near old battlefields.

name: Bottled Sea-foam
description: Captured at dawn on Thassa's shores. Used in potions of water breathing and calm.

name: Sprig of Wild Thyme
description: Common kitchen-and-cure herb. Mild antiseptic.

name: Goat's Tallow (rendered)
description: Base for salves and ointments. Smells faintly of the pasture.

name: Dried Pomegranate Seeds
description: Sacred to Erebos. Used in necromantic ink and contracts with the dead.

name: Hydra Scale (small)
description: A single iridescent scale, palm-sized. Component for potions of regrowth and resistance to acid.

name: Cyclops Eyelash
description: Thick as a quill. Used in true-seeing draughts and bullseye lanterns.

name: Satyr Horn Shavings
description: Trimmings ritually given, never taken. Component for potions of charm and revelry.

name: Pegasus Feather
description: Pure white quill. Levitation and feather-fall reagent.

name: Phoenix Ash (pinch)
description: From a captured spark of Purphoros's forge. Used in potions of fire resistance.

name: Cockatrice Beak (shard)
description: Petrifaction reagent — handle with leather gloves only.

name: Returned Bone-dust
description: Sacred-or-blasphemous depending on which priest you ask. Component for potions of false life.

name: Sphinx Whisker
description: Vanishes if spoken of carelessly. Component for divinations and potions of clairvoyance.

name: Drop of Nyx-mist (sealed phial)
description: Starry vapor caught at the edge of Nyx. Used in any dreaming or prophecy draught.

name: Adult Hydra Tooth
description: Heavy, serrated. Component for potions of regeneration.

name: Medusa Hair (single strand)
description: Still faintly twitching. Petrify-resistance reagent; storage in lead vial mandatory.

name: Tear of a Naiad
description: Crystallized to a tiny bead. Component for water-walk and purify-water draughts.

name: Chimera Mane Hair
description: Three colors braided. Used in elixirs of multi-resistance.

name: Potion of Healing
description: Restores 2d4+2 HP. Theros apothecaries dye theirs faint red with crushed pomegranate.

name: Potion of Climbing
description: Climb speed equal to walking speed for 1 hour. Common gift for hoplites raiding cliffside temples.

name: Potion of Watchful Rest
description: Long rest with one eye open — no exhaustion from short watches. Popular on the roads.

name: Potion of Greater Healing
description: Restores 4d4+4 HP. Standard issue for polis hoplite physicians.

name: Potion of Water Breathing
description: Breathe water for 1 hour. Bottled in Meletis with a sliver of coral floating inside.

name: Potion of Animal Friendship
description: Cast Animal Friendship at will for 1 hour. Druidic, with a sprig of wild laurel.

name: Potion of Hill Giant Strength
description: Strength 21 for 1 hour. Smells faintly of crushed boulders.

name: Potion of Resistance (fire)
description: Resistance to fire damage for 1 hour. Forge-priest issue.

name: Potion of Heroism
description: 10 temp HP + Bless for 1 hour. Sold near every temple of Iroas.

name: Potion of Mind Reading
description: Cast Detect Thoughts on drinking. Banned in three poleis.

name: Potion of Superior Healing
description: Restores 8d4+8 HP. Stored under wax seal of Karametra.

name: Potion of Invisibility
description: Invisible for 1 hour or until you attack/cast. A favorite of Phenax's faithful.

name: Potion of Speed
description: Haste spell effect for 1 minute. Includes the post-effect lethargy — drink wisely.

name: Potion of Gaseous Form
description: Gaseous form for 1 hour. Glass phial; broken seal evaporates immediately.

name: Potion of Stone Giant Strength
description: Strength 23 for 1 hour. Heavy in the hand even before drinking.

name: Loaf of Coarse Bread
description: Daily staple. 2 cp at any village bakery.

name: Loaf of Honeyed Bread
description: Drizzled with Karametran honey; keeps for two days.

name: Wheel of Goat Cheese
description: Sharp and pungent. Travels well wrapped in waxed cloth.

name: Wheel of Aged Sheep Cheese
description: Premium polis fare. A wedding gift among Meletian merchants.

name: Smoked Octopus (skewer)
description: Coastal favorite. Two skewers feed one for a day.

name: Salted Tuna (1 lb)
description: Brick-firm, will keep three weeks on the road.

name: Dried Figs (1 lb)
description: Sweet, light, and traveler-friendly.

name: Jar of Olives (1 lb)
description: In brine. Theran staple — every household has a jar.

name: Bottle of Common Wine
description: Pitcher of rough red wine, slightly watered. Standard tavern fare.

name: Bottle of Fine Wine (Meletian)
description: Vintage from the temple-slopes. Gift-worthy.

name: Amphora of Olive Oil (1 gal)
description: Cooking, lamp fuel, and ritual anointing in one.

name: Jar of Honey (1 lb)
description: Karametra's gift. Sweetens wine, cures ills, attracts bees.

name: Pomegranate (whole)
description: A coin a piece in season. Sacred fruit of Erebos and oaths.

name: Bag of Almonds (1 lb)
description: Roasted with salt. Traveling rations.

name: Bag of Salt (1 lb)
description: 5 cp. Preserves meat, blesses thresholds.

name: Travel Rations (1 day)
description: Hardtack, dried meat, dried fruit. Standard PHB pricing.

name: Trail Rations (1 week)
description: Seven days of travel food, packed in oilcloth.

name: Fresh Catch of the Day (1 lb)
description: Whatever the morning boats brought in. Eat today.

name: Live Chicken
description: A clucking copper. Three eat for a day from one bird.

name: Live Goat
description: Walks itself to your table. Cheap protein for a small party.

name: Side of Mutton
description: A whole side of salted mutton. Feeds 10 for a week.

name: Hardtack Biscuit (each)
description: Indestructible. Soldiers' fare.

name: Smoked Sausage (link)
description: Spiced with thyme and oregano. Theros polis specialty.

name: Jar of Garum (fish sauce)
description: Fermented fish sauce. Polis cooks swear by it; villagers find it foul.

name: Crock of Yogurt
description: Tart and cool. Eaten with honey at breakfast.

name: Crock of Pickled Olives
description: Brine-cured for a year. Pairs with everything.

name: Bushel of Wheat
description: Bulk grain. A bushel feeds a small family a week.

name: Loaf of Barley Bread
description: Heavier and cheaper than wheat. Rural staple.

name: Sack of Dried Lentils (5 lb)
description: Cheap protein. Will keep a year if dry.

name: Pot of Bee-bread
description: Honey, pollen, ground grain — concentrated traveler's food. Three bites is a meal.

name: Iron Cauldron
description: Heavy three-legged pot for the hearth.

name: Bronze Cooking Skillet
description: Polis kitchens use these for everything from fish to flatbread.

name: Mess Kit
description: Tin cup, plate, and utensil. PHB standard.

name: Wineskin (full)
description: Holds 4 pints. Buy filled.

name: Bronze Wine Cup
description: Cast bronze; lasts a lifetime.

name: Set of Carving Knives
description: Three blades on a roll. Cook's kit.

name: Stone Mortar and Pestle
description: For grinding salt, spices, and grain.

name: Bronze Cooking Tripod
description: Folds flat for travel. Holds a pot over a campfire.

name: Olive-wood Spoon (set of 4)
description: Carved by village artisans.

name: Clay Amphora (empty, 1 gal)
description: Standard storage for oil, wine, or grain.

name: Bronze Brazier (small)
description: Portable cooking brazier. Polis market staple.

name: Sack of Spice Mix (1 lb)
description: Coriander, oregano, cumin, mountain saffron. Polis cooks pay dearly.

name: Sealed Pot of Garlic Confit
description: Whole garlic slow-cooked in olive oil. Keeps a season; spreads on bread.

name: Mule
description: Sure-footed. Carries 420 lb. The villager's friend.

name: Donkey
description: Slower than a mule but eats less. Carries 420 lb.

name: Ox
description: Pulls plow or wagon. Slow but tireless.

name: Pony
description: Carries 225 lb. Good for hill villages and small riders.

name: Draft Horse
description: Pulls heavy loads. Polis wagon-trains run on these.

name: Riding Horse
description: Standard mount. Trots all day if cared for.

name: Warhorse
description: Trained for combat. Hoplite cavalry standard. Premium markets only.

name: Camel (rare import)
description: Imported from Akros's far trade. Drinks once a week.

name: Trained Stag (Nylea's gift)
description: Sacred to Nylea. Carries 350 lb, faster than a horse in forest. Sale taboo in some villages.

name: Riding Centaur Companion (contract)
description: Centaurs are people, not mounts. This stall posts contracts with centaur scouts willing to bear gear — typical fee 1 gp/day.

name: Tame Lamassu Calf (very rare)
description: Half-bull, half-lion, sky-blessed. Only one polis breeder licensed to sell.

name: Pegasus Foal (extremely rare, license required)
description: Sale requires a writ from a temple of Heliod. Most stables only have one for show.

name: Cart
description: Two wheels, one ox or pony. Carries 200 lb.

name: Wagon
description: Four wheels, two-horse team. Carries 600 lb.

name: Carriage
description: Enclosed, suspension. Polis-noble transport.

name: War Chariot (two-horse)
description: Bronze-rimmed wheels, hoplite-style. Banned inside many polis walls.

name: Sled
description: For snowy passes north of Akros.

name: Rowboat
description: Coastal villages use these by the dozen.

name: Sailing Skiff
description: Single-mast, two crew. Hops between islands.

name: Longship (small trireme)
description: Twenty-oared. Polis charters only.

name: Saddle, Riding
description: Standard leather saddle for any horse or pony.

name: Saddle, Military
description: Higher cantle, holds a rider on through a charge.

name: Saddle, Pack
description: Straps for mules and donkeys.

name: Saddle, Exotic (pegasus/lamassu)
description: Specially shaped for winged or unusual mounts.

name: Bit and Bridle
description: Standard tack.

name: Saddlebags (pair)
description: Holds 30 lb per side.

name: Barding, Leather (riding horse)
description: x4 base saddle cost rule of thumb. Light protection.

name: Barding, Chain (warhorse)
description: Polis cavalry standard. Heavy and loud.

name: Feed (1 day)
description: 5 cp. Hay and grain for one beast.

name: Stable Fee (1 day)
description: Polis stable, watered and rubbed down.

name: Caravan Guard Hire (1 day)
description: One hoplite escort. Per person, per day.

name: Wheelwright's Spare Wheel
description: Always carry one on long journeys.

name: Whip
description: Cracks loud. PHB simple weapon.

name: Goad (long stick)
description: 1 sp. For driving stubborn oxen.

name: Horse Brush & Pick Kit
description: Basic care kit. Lasts a season.

name: Travel Blanket (mount)
description: Wool blanket sized for a horse.

name: Trained Falcon
description: Theros peregrine falcon. Pinpoint stoop and reliable recall. The hoplite scout's eye in the sky.

name: Trained Owl (messenger)
description: Sacred to Kruphix; rare on the market. Carries small letters by night.

name: Trained Eagle (Heliod's blessing)
description: Golden eagle from the high crags. Hunts goats, attacks wolves. Polis-issue only.

name: Trained Pegasus Hatchling (theros rare)
description: Treated as falconry only in jest — a fledgling pegasus, not for hunting. Temple-licensed sale only.

name: Falconry Gauntlet
description: Thick leather. Saves the wrist from talons.

name: Falcon Hood
description: Calms the bird in transit. Embroidered ones go for double.

name: Bird Perch (portable)
description: Folds for travel. T-shape with a padded top.

name: Bird Perch (kitchen/stable)
description: Stationary stand. Polis household fixture.

name: Jesses & Bells (set)
description: Leather straps and small brass bells. Helps locate the bird on the wing.

name: Lure (training)
description: Feathered weight on a cord. For recall training.

name: Bird Cage (woven)
description: For small birds. Wicker with a bronze hinge.

name: Bird Cage (ornate, polis)
description: Brass and olive-wood. A status symbol.

name: Bag of Live Feed (week of mice)
description: 5 sp. Fresh prey for a single falcon for a week.

name: Bag of Smoked Quail (feed)
description: Travel feed. Keeps a falcon healthy for two weeks.

name: Falconer's Whistle (bronze)
description: Three-tone bronze. Standard recall signal.

name: Telemetry Bell (charmed)
description: Faintly enchanted bronze bell. Rings in the falconer's ear when the bird is in distress, up to 1 mile.

name: Falconry Manual (illustrated)
description: A polis scribe's guide. Grants advantage on Animal Handling checks with raptors after one week of study.

name: Bird-keeper's License (polis)
description: Required in Meletis to keep a hunting bird inside the walls. Annual fee.
